article_meta.title: Border czar Tom Homan on Thursday invited Catholic leadership to watch him and U.S

reasoning: The summary is mostly neutral and attributes charged claims to Homan or Catholic leaders. Minor lapses include describing "record removals" without attribution and calling the approach "aggressive enforcement," which imports a subjective descriptor. Otherwise it avoids adopting the source’s loaded framing and presents multiple perspectives.

custom_reasoning.spin: Mostly neutral verbs; one subjective phrase ("aggressive enforcement") and un-attributed "record removals."
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: States "record removals" and implies scope of agent increases without explicit attribution; child exploitation claims are mostly attributed.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Uses a couple of evaluative terms ("aggressive," "significant") in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarming language beyond summarizing claims.
custom_reasoning.slant: Focus tracks the source, giving space to Homan and noting Catholic critiques; no undue emphasis.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes all major perspectives mentioned; no key view omitted.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Generally neutral; political terms appear in attributed context.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced coverage of criticism and defense.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No systematic favoring of elites or grassroots.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Largely neutral paraphrase with minor loaded phrasing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures Homan/ICE, bishops, pope, and archdiocese; slight gap in not listing the parish as a distinct perspective.
